steps.title: Find the comparison signal
steps.content: The goal is to compare how Schubart and Charpentier each describe the mood of C minor, so the correct choice must include both of their specific descriptions.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice B — Schubart describes the mood of C minor as expressing "longing," while Charpentier describes it as "obscure and sad." gives Schubart’s description of C minor as expressing longing and Charpentier’s description as obscure and sad, linked by 'while' to show a direct comparison.
The other choices either omit one composer’s description, introduce unrelated information, or only define the key without any mood comparison.
That matches Choice B — Schubart describes the mood of C minor as expressing "longing," while Charpentier describes it as "obscure and sad.".
